---
title: "Creativity-AI (v2, post reviews)"
author: "Dan Dediu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document: 
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 6
    toc_float:
      collapsed: yes
      smooth_scroll: no
    highlight: tango
    theme: cerulean
    code_folding: hide
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
bibliography: bibliography.bib
csl: apa-6th-edition.csl
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=TRUE, cache=TRUE);
```

```{r libraries and initializations, results='hide', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Rmarkdown:
library(knitr);
library(pander);

# Plotting:
library(reshape2);
library(dplyr);
library(ggplot2);
library(gridExtra);
library(sjPlot);

# Psychometrics:
library(performance);
library(factoextra);
library(NbClust);
library(mokken);
library(psych);
library(lavaan);
library(lavaanPlot);

# Regression:
library(lmerTest);
library(glmmTMB);
#library(brms);
library(effects);
library(emmeans);

# System/parallel processing:
library(parallel);

# Misc:
library(benchmarkme);

if(!dir.exists("./cache")) dir.create("./cache", showWarnings=FALSE, recursive=TRUE);

# For parallel processing:
n_cores <- ifelse(Sys.info()['sysname'] == "Windows", 1, max(detectCores(all.tests=TRUE, logical=FALSE)-1, 1, na.rm=TRUE)); # try to use multiple cores, if running on a non-Windows system

## Set seed for reproducibility:
set.seed(42);

# Figure and Table caption adapted from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37116632/rmarkdown-html-number-figures: 
outputFormat = opts_knit$get("rmarkdown.pandoc.to"); # determine the output format of the document
if( is.null(outputFormat) ) outputFormat = ""; # probably not run within knittr
capTabNo = 1; capFigNo = 1; # figure and table caption numbering, for HTML do it manually
#Function to add the Table Number
capTab = function(x, supplementary=FALSE, id=TRUE)
{
  if(outputFormat == 'html'){
    #x = paste0("<div class='caption'>**Table ",ifelse(supplementary,"S",""),capTabNo,".** ",x,"</div>")
    x = paste0("**Table ",ifelse(supplementary,"S",""),capTabNo,".** ",x,"")
    if( id ) x <- paste0(x, "<a id='Tab",ifelse(supplementary,"S",""),capTabNo,"'/>");
    capTabNo <<- capTabNo + 1
  }; x
}
#Function to add the Figure Number
capFig = function(x, show_R_version=TRUE, show_package_versions=NULL, is_map=FALSE, supplementary=FALSE, id=TRUE)
{
  if(outputFormat == 'html')
  {
    x <- paste0("**Figure ",ifelse(supplementary,"S",""),capFigNo,".** ",x,"");
    if( show_R_version || (!is.null(show_package_versions) && length(show_package_versions) > 0) )
    {
      x <- paste0(x, " Figure generated using ");
      if( show_R_version ) x <- paste0(x, stringr::str_replace(R.version.string, stringr::fixed("R "), "[`R`](https://www.r-project.org/) "));
      if( !is.null(show_package_versions) && length(show_package_versions) > 0 )
      {
        x <- paste0(x, ifelse( show_R_version, " and ", " "));
        x <- paste0(x, ifelse( length(show_package_versions) > 1, "packages ", "package "));
        x <- paste0(x, paste0(vapply(show_package_versions, function(x) paste0("`",x,"`", " (version ", packageVersion(x),")"), character(1)), collapse=", "), ".");
      }
      if( is_map ) x <- paste0(x, " Maps are using public domain data from the [Natural Earth project](https://www.naturalearthdata.com/) as provided by the `R` package `maps`.");
      if( id ) x <- paste0(x, "<a id='Fig",ifelse(supplementary,"S",""),capFigNo,"'/>");
    }
    capFigNo <<- capFigNo + 1;
  }; 
  x;
}


# Define a function to partition an item set into mokken scales - lowerbound from .05 to .60 
# (this tells R to apply aisp to any given data.frame for a set of lowerbounds if you call moscales.for.lowerbounds)
# ^^^^^^^ change .60 value to higher if you need to see how items behave at higher levels of c 
# DD: parallelize it and add handle "Error in aisp(x, lowerbound = lowerbound) : no lowerbound provided"
moscales.for.lowerbounds <- function(x, lowerbounds=seq(from=0.05,to=0.60,by=0.05), ncores=1)
{
  if( ncores == 1 )
  {
    # run the original version:
    ret.value <- NULL;
    for( lowerbound in lowerbounds )
    {
      tmp <- aisp( x,  lowerbound=lowerbound );
      if( is.null(ret.value) )
      {
        ret.value <- data.frame( "Item"=rownames(tmp), "Scales."=tmp[,1] );
      }
      else
      {
        ret.value <- cbind( ret.value, "Scales."=tmp[,1] );
      }
      names(ret.value)[ncol(ret.value)] <- paste("c=",sprintf("%.2f",lowerbound),sep="");
    }
    rownames(ret.value) <- NULL;
    ret.value;
  } else
  {
    # run the parallel version:
    ret.value <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, mclapply(lowerbounds, function(lowerbound)
    {
      tmp <- NULL;
      try(tmp <- mokken::aisp( x,  lowerbound=lowerbound ), silent=TRUE);
      if( is.null(tmp) ){ return (rep(NA,ncol(x))) } else { return (tmp[,1]) }
    }, mc.cores=ncores)));
    names(ret.value) <- sprintf("c=%.2f",lowerbounds);
    return (ret.value);
  }
}

```


# Questionnaire structure

## Loading and pre-processing the data

```{r results='hide'}
# load the data:
dhum  <- read.csv(xzfile("./data/processed/ratings-human.csv.xz"), sep="\t"); # human ratings
dgpt  <- read.csv(xzfile("./data/processed/ratings-ai.csv.xz"), sep="\t");    # GTP ratings
dggpt <- read.csv("./data/processed/guided-gpt-ratings.csv", sep="\t");       # human-guided GPT ratings

# let's extract and rename/recode the items:
ditems <- unique(rbind(dhum[,c("category", "imageId")], dgpt[,c("category", "imageId")]));
nrow(ditems); # 1000 - fine!
ditems$subcategory <- ifelse(ditems$category  == "Human Inspired GenAI", "HAI",
                          ifelse(ditems$category  == "Self-Guided GenAI", "SAI",
                                 ifelse(ditems$category  == "Visual Artists", "HVA",
                                        ifelse(ditems$category  == "Non Artists", "HNA",
                                               NA))));
ditems$category <- ifelse(ditems$category %in% c("Human Inspired GenAI", "Self-Guided GenAI"), "AI", "H");
ditems <- ditems[ order(ditems$category, ditems$subcategory, ditems$imageId), ];
ditems$iID <- vapply(1:nrow(ditems), function(i) sprintf("%s%03d", ditems$subcategory[i], which(ditems$imageId[i] == ditems$imageId[ ditems$subcategory == ditems$subcategory[i] ])), character(1));

# add the new item ids to the databases:
dhum <- merge(dhum, ditems, by="imageId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
dhum <- cbind("judge"="human", dhum[,c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity",  "participantId")]);
names(dhum) <- c("judgeType", "itemID", "category", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity", "judgeId");
dgpt <- merge(dgpt, ditems, by="imageId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
dgpt <- cbind("judge"="AI", dgpt[,c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity")],  "participantId"="ai");
names(dgpt) <- c("judgeType", "itemID", "category", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity", "judgeId");
dggpt <- merge(dggpt, ditems, by="imageId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
dggpt <- cbind("judge"="gAI", dggpt[,c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity")],  "participantId"="gai");
names(dggpt) <- c("judgeType", "itemID", "category", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity", "judgeId");

# merge the datasets, also adding the new item info:
dall <- rbind(dhum, dgpt, dggpt);

# add the human raters info:
draters <- read.csv(xzfile("./data/processed/human-raters-info.csv.xz"), sep="\t");
draters$areaScore <- round(as.numeric(draters$areaScore),2);
dall <- merge(dall, unique(draters[,c("participantId", "age", "gender", "yearsEducation", "areaScore")]), by.x="judgeId", by.y="participantId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
names(dall)[11:14] <- c("judgeAge", "judgeGender", "judgeYearsEducation", "judgeAreaScore");
dall <- dall[,c("itemID", "category", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity", "judgeId", "judgeType", "judgeAge", "judgeGender", "judgeYearsEducation", "judgeAreaScore")];

# add the drawing info for the AI-generated drawings:
daidraw <- read.csv("./data/processed/drawings-info-ai.csv", sep="\t");
daidraw <- merge(daidraw, ditems, by="imageId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
daidraw <- daidraw[,c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory", "stimulus")]; names(daidraw) <- c("itemID", "category", "subcategory", "stimulus");
dall <- merge(dall, daidraw, by=c("itemID", "category", "subcategory"), all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);

# add the drawing and drawer info for the human-drawn drawings:
dhumdraw <- read.csv("./data/processed/drawings-and-drawers-info-human.csv", sep="\t");
dhumdraw$areaScore <- round(as.numeric(dhumdraw$areaScore),2);
dhumdraw <- merge(dhumdraw, ditems, by="imageId", all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE);
dall <- merge(dall, dhumdraw[,c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory", "stimulus", "participantId", "age", "gender", "educationLevel", "artisticField", "numYearsArt", "areaScore")], 
              by.x=c("itemID", "category", "subcategory"), by.y=c("iID", "category.y", "subcategory"), all.x=TRUE, all.y=FALSE); 
stopifnot(!any(!is.na(dall$stimulus.x) & !is.na(dall$stimulus.y))); # sanity check: the human and AI drawers should be complementary
dall$stimulus.x[ is.na(dall$stimulus.x) ] <- dall$stimulus.y[ is.na(dall$stimulus.x) ];
dall <- dall[,c("itemID", "category", "subcategory", "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics", "curiosity", "judgeId", "judgeType", "judgeAge", "judgeGender", "judgeYearsEducation", "judgeAreaScore", "stimulus.x", "participantId", "age", "gender", "educationLevel", "artisticField", "numYearsArt", "areaScore")]; 

# use shorter names for the variables (where appropriate)
names(dall) <- c("iid", "cat", "subcat", "liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "jid", "jtyp", "jage", "jgen", "jeduc", "jasc", "stim", "did", "dage", "dgen", "deduc", "dfield", "dart", "dasc"); 

# make the variables factors with the right contrasts:
dall$iid    <- factor(dall$iid);
dall$cat    <- factor(dall$cat, levels=c("H", "AI"));
dall$subcat <- factor(dall$subcat, levels=c("HVA", "HNA", "HAI", "SAI"));
dall$jid    <- factor(dall$jid);
dall$jtyp   <- factor(dall$jtyp, levels=c("human", "AI", "gAI"));
dall$jgen   <- factor(dall$jgen, levels=c("Female", "Male", "Non-binary", "Other"));
dall$stim   <- factor(dall$stim);
dall$did    <- factor(dall$did);
dall$dgen   <- factor(dall$dgen, levels=c("Female", "Male"));
dall$deduc[ dall$deduc %in% c("Bachelors", "Bachelor’s degree", "Bachelor Degree", "Bachelor Degree (grado superior)") ] <- "Bachelors"; dall$deduc  <- factor(dall$deduc, levels=c("High School diploma", "Bachelors", "Master's degree"));
dall$dfield[ !is.na(dall$dfield) & dall$dfield == "" ] <- NA; dall$dfield <- factor(dall$dfield);


# ... and put also in long format:
dall.long <- dall %>% melt(measure.vars=c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios"), variable.name="question", value.name="value");

# ... make some of the categorical variables numeric as well:
dall$jtypb   <- as.numeric(dall$jtyp)-1;
dall$catb    <- as.numeric(dall$cat)-1;
dall$subcatb <- as.numeric(dall$subcat %in% c("HVA", "HAI"));

# free the memory:
rm(dhum, dgpt, dggpt, draters, daidraw, dhumdraw); gc();
```


## Items, judges and conditions

Here we look at the (psychometric) structure of the creativity judgment questionnaire.

This is composed of 5 scales (here called "questions"), "liking", "vividness", "originality", "aesthetics" and "curiosity", denoted henceforth as *liking*, *vivid*, *origin*, *aesth* and *curios*, each measured on a 7-steps Likert scale from 1 ("not at all") to 7 ("extremely"), applied to each of the `nrow(ditems)` drawings (here called "items").

There are three conditions: one involving human raters (henceforth, *human*), another AI ratings (*ai*), and the last involving guided AI ratings (*gai*) which are designed to be as comparable as possible but, given the objective characteristics of the situations, are not identical:

- *human* involved a sample of `r length(unique(dall$jid[dall$jtyp=="human"]))` raters with various characteristics (sex, age...), each rater rating `r mean(table(as.character(dall$jid[ dall$jtyp=="human" ])))` drawings (not all the same, to avoid fatigue, but in such a way as to make sure that each drawing was rated by at least `r min(table(as.character(dall$iid[ dall$jtyp=="human" ])))` judges):

```{r fig.width=5, fig.height=4, fig.cap=capFig("Histogram of how many items have been rated by how many human judges.")}
hist(table(dall$iid[ dall$jtyp=="human" ]), xlab="# human judges", ylab="number of drawings", main=NULL);
```

- *ai* and *gai* involved prompting, for each drawing, ChatGPT to rate one of the five questions on the same Likert scale that the humans raters used, repeatedly for `r mean(table(as.character(dall$iid[ dall$jtyp=="AI" ])))` times. 



## Variables

All in all, we have the following information, organized by a drawing (or *item*) being judged by a given judge:

- `iid`: its unique identifier, composed of its subcategory (see below) and a unique number within the category (factor)
- `cat`: a factor with two levels, "H" and "AI" denoting how the drawing was generated (by a human or by AI, respectively)
- `subcat`: a factor with four levels making further distinctions within each category: for "H" it distinguishes between naive ("HNA") and artists ("HVA"), and for "AI", between human-inspired ("HAI") and self-generated ("SAI")
- `liking`, `vivid`, `origin`, `aesth`, `curios`: the ratings on the five 1-7 Likert scales (numeric)
- `jid` and `jtyp`: the judge's (scorer's) unique ID (if human) or "ai"/"gai" (if judged by AI), and it's type ("H" for human and "AI"/"gAI" for AI) (factor)
- `jage`, `jgen`, `jeduc` and `jasc`: for a human judge, their age (in years, numeric), gender (factor), years of education (numeric) and the judge's AReA (Aesthetic Responsiveness to Art) score (numeric)
- `stim`: the stimulus used to obtain the drawing (factor)
- `did`, `dage`, `dgen`, `deduc`, `dfield`, `dart`, `dasc`: the drawer info for human drawers: unique ID (factor), age (numeric), gender (factor), level of education (factor with three levels), artistic field of activity for the visual artists (factor) and the number of years of artistic activity (numeric), and the drawer's AReA (Aesthetic Responsiveness to Art) score (numeric)
- `jtypb`, `catb` and `subcatb`: numeric versions of `jtyp`, `cat` and `subcat` to be used in certain analyses that require numeric data.



## Distribution of responses

Let's see now how the responses to the 5 questions look like.

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=1*5, fig.cap=capFig("Histograms comparing the scores on the five scales between AI and human raters across categories."), message=FALSE}
ggplot(dall.long, aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For all drawings together");
```

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Histograms comparing the scores on the five scales between AI and human raters for specific categories and sub-categories."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$cat=="H", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For human drawings"),
             ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$subcat=="HNA", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For visual artist drawings"),
             ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$subcat=="HVA", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For naive human drawings"),
             ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$cat=="AI", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For AI drawings"),
             ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$subcat=="SAI", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For self-generated AI drawings"),
             ggplot(dall.long[ dall.long$subcat=="HAI", ], aes(x=value)) + 
               geom_histogram(aes(fill=jtyp)) + 
               facet_grid(jtyp ~ question) + theme(legend.position="none") + ggtitle("For human-inspired AI drawings"),
             ncol=3);
```
```{r results='hide'}
rm(dall.long); gc();
```

It is absolutely clear that the human and AI raters have very different distributions of their answers on the 1--7 Likert scales, with the AI responses resembling a peaked symmetric distribution centered around the middle of the scale, while human responses are asymmetrically distributed, monotonically decreasing from 1 towards 7, suggesting that the processes underlying the human and AI judgments are **fundamentally different**.
However, the guided AI responses are *somewhat in between*, but still more similar to AI than to human responses.


## Clustering of the scales

Below we show the correlation matrix (with clustering) for the judgments on the 5 scales and the numerically coded category (0=human, 1=AI), separately for the human judges (left) and the AI judge (right):

```{r fig.width=3*2.5, fig.height=1*5, fig.cap=capFig("Correlations matrix with clustering for the human judges only. Left: all drawings by category; middle: only human drawings by subcateogry; right: only AI drawings by subcateogry. The variables are: *catb* = category (0=human, 1=AI), *subcatb* = subcategory (within human, 0=naive, 1=artist; within AI: 0=self, 1=human-inspired), *lik*=liking, *viv*=vividness, *ori*=originality, *aes*=aesthetics, *cur*=curiosity."), fig.show="hold", out.width="33%"}
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="human", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "catb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),
                  cellnote=round(d,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Human judges");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="human" & dall$cat=="H", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"), 
                  cellnote=round(d,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Human judges on humans");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="human" & dall$cat=="AI", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                       method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),  
                   cellnote=round(d,2),  notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Human judges on AI");
```
```{r fig.width=3*2.5, fig.height=1*5, fig.cap=capFig("Correlations matrix with clustering for the AI judges only. Left: all drawings by category; middle: only human drawings by subcateogry; right: only AI drawings by subcateogry. The variables are: *catb* = category (0=human, 1=AI), *subcatb* = subcategory (within human, 0=naive, 1=artist; within AI: 0=self, 1=human-inspired), *lik*=liking, *viv*=vividness, *ori*=originality, *aes*=aesthetics, *cur*=curiosity."), fig.show="hold", out.width="33%"}
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="AI", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "catb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),
                  cellnote=round(d ,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="AI judges");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="AI" & dall$cat=="H", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"), 
                  cellnote=round(d,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="AI judges on humans");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="AI" & dall$cat=="AI", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),
                  cellnote=round(d,2),  notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="AI judges on AI");
```
```{r fig.width=3*2.5, fig.height=1*5, fig.cap=capFig("Correlations matrix with clustering for the guided AI judges only. Left: all drawings by category; middle: only human drawings by subcateogry; right: only AI drawings by subcateogry. The variables are: *catb* = category (0=human, 1=AI), *subcatb* = subcategory (within human, 0=naive, 1=artist; within AI: 0=self, 1=human-inspired), *lik*=liking, *viv*=vividness, *ori*=originality, *aes*=aesthetics, *cur*=curiosity."), fig.show="hold", out.width="33%"}
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="gAI", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "catb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),
                  cellnote=round(d ,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Guided AI judges");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="gAI" & dall$cat=="H", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"), 
                  cellnote=round(d,2), notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Guided AI judges on humans");
gplots::heatmap.2(d <- cor(dall[ dall$jtyp=="gAI" & dall$cat=="AI", c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios", "subcatb")], 
                      method="pearson", use="pairwise.complete.obs"),
                  cellnote=round(d,2),  notecol="black", margins=c(4,4), main="Guided AI judges on AI");
```
```{r results='hide'}
# free the memory:
gc();
```

Again, it is clear that the structure if very different between humans and AI:

- for *humans* and *gAI*, the human drawing are consistently rated as having higher scores on all 5 scales (negative correlations of *catb* with all scales), while for the *ai*, human drawings are scored higher only on *aesthetics*, *likeness* and *vividness*,
- *humans* and *gAI* clearly prefer the drawings of experts (to those of the novices) and of human-inspired AI (to the self-generated AI), while the *AI* seems to not really make a differences between human artists and naive people (sightly preferring the latter), but strongly prefers the human-inspired to the self-generated AI ones,
- the scales cluster in the same way for the *AI* across categories as ((*originality*, *curiosity*), ((*aesthetics*, *liking*), *vividness*)), for *gAI* as ((((*aesthetics*, *liking*), *curiosity*), *vividness*), *originality*), while for the *human* judges, the structure is different, being ((*originality*, *vividness*), ((*aesthetics*, *curiosity*), *liking*)) for the human drawings, and ((*originality*, *curiosity*), ((*aesthetics*, *liking*), *vividness*)) for the AI generated drawings.

While impressionistic, these finds seem to reinforce the ideas that the human and AI judge the human-generated drawings using different criteria, but the AI-generated drawings using similar criteria, and that gAI is somewhat more similar to humans than to AI.


## Clustering of the ratings

Let's see how many clusters there might be in the data:

```{r results='hide', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(parameters);

# if( !file.exists("./cache/dclust.rds") )
# {
  # computationally very expensive: do it and save it!
  dall.scales <- dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")];
  
  # # elbow (fails for the whole dataset):
  # dall.clust.elbow <- n_clusters_elbow(dall.scales);
  # dall.clust.elbow;
  # plot(dall.clust.elbow);
  
  # gap:
  #dall.clust.gap <- n_clusters_gap(dall.scales); # extrmeely slow even on a very powerful machine
  #dall.clust.gap;
  #plot(dall.clust.gap); # between 4 and 6 seems ok
  
  # silhouette:
  #dall.clust.sil <- n_clusters_silhouette(dall.scales); # crashes for all the items ("long vectors (argument 1) are not supported in .C")
  #dall.clust.sil;
  #plot(dall.clust.sil); # 2 clusters
  
  dall.kmeans.2 <- kmeans(dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")], 2, nstart=100);
  dall.kmeans.3 <- kmeans(dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")], 3, nstart=100);
  dall.kmeans.4 <- kmeans(dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")], 4, nstart=100);
  dall.kmeans.5 <- kmeans(dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")], 5, nstart=100);
  dall.kmeans.6 <- kmeans(dall[, c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")], 6, nstart=100);
  
  dall.kmeans <- cbind(dall, 
                       "k2"=as.factor(paste0("cluster ", 1:2)[dall.kmeans.2$cluster]),
                       "k3"=as.factor(paste0("cluster ", 1:3)[dall.kmeans.3$cluster]),
                       "k4"=as.factor(paste0("cluster ", 1:4)[dall.kmeans.4$cluster]),
                       "k5"=as.factor(paste0("cluster ", 1:5)[dall.kmeans.5$cluster]),
                       "k6"=as.factor(paste0("cluster ", 1:6)[dall.kmeans.6$cluster]));
  
  dclust <- list(#"gap"=dall.clust.gap, "silhouette"=dall.clust.sil, 
                 "kmeans"=dall.kmeans);
  
#   saveRDS(dclust, file="./cache/dclust.rds", compress="xz");
# }

# z-score the scales scores:
dclust$kmeans$likingz <- scale(dclust$kmeans$liking, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE);
dclust$kmeans$vividz  <- scale(dclust$kmeans$vivid, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE);
dclust$kmeans$originz <- scale(dclust$kmeans$origin, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE);
dclust$kmeans$aesthz  <- scale(dclust$kmeans$aesth, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE);
dclust$kmeans$curiosz <- scale(dclust$kmeans$curios, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE);
```

```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE, fig.width=2*4, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("The optimal number of clusters in the entire data using the gap method (left) and the silhouette method (right)."), fig.show="hold", out.width="50%"}
plot(dclust$gap); # between 4 and 6 seems ok
plot(dclust$silhouette); # 2 clusters
```

### k=2 clusters

#### How do judge and category affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of responses by cluster and various criteria.")}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k2, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k2, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k2, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ cat) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k2, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k2, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ subcat) +
               geom_text(aes(x=k2, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ncol=3);
```

Cluster 1 seems to contain mostly the human and gAI judgments, while cluster 2 mostly the AI judgments relatively similarly across categories (human- or AI-generated) and subcategories, but let's test this taking into account the proper structure of the data (i.e., random effect of item and judge).

```{r}
# only jtyp:
if( !file.exists("./cache/glmer_mk2j.rds") )
{
  mk2.j <- glmer(k2 ~ jtyp + (1 | iid) + (1 | jid), data=dclust$kmeans, family=binomial);
  #summary(mk2.j);
  saveRDS(mk2.j, file="./cache/glmer_mk2j.rds", compress="xz");
  mk2.0 <- update(mk2.j, . ~ . - jtyp); anova(mk2.j, mk2.0); # judge type does not seem to have a main effect...
  saveRDS(mk2.0, file="./cache/glmer_mk20.rds", compress="xz");
} else
{
  mk2.0 <- readRDS("./cache/glmer_mk20.rds");
  mk2.j <- readRDS("./cache/glmer_mk2j.rds");
}
summary(mk2.j);
icc(mk2.j); # 75%
plot_model(mk2.j, type="pred", terms=c("jtyp")) + ylab("p(cluster 2)") + ggtitle("Pred. probs. for k=2"); # because gAI is all over the place!
```

So, it seems that the judge type does not have a main effect, basically because AI and especially gAI is all over the place...

```{r}
# jtyp & cat:
if( !file.exists("./cache/glmer_mk2jc.rds") )
{
  mk2.jc <- glmer(k2 ~ jtyp * cat + (1 | iid) + (1 | jid), data=dclust$kmeans, family=binomial);
  #summary(mk2.jc);
  mk2.jc.noint <- update(mk2.jc, . ~ . - jtyp:cat); anova(mk2.jc, mk2.jc.noint); # the interaction clearly matters!
  saveRDS(mk2.jc, file="./cache/glmer_mk2jc.rds", compress="xz");
} else
{
  mk2.jc <- readRDS("./cache/glmer_mk2jc.rds");
}
summary(mk2.jc);
anova(mk2.jc, mk2.0); # and improves on the null model!
anova(mk2.jc, mk2.j); # and on the judge-only model
plot_model(mk2.jc, type="pred", terms=c("jtyp", "cat")) + ylab("p(cluster 2)") + ggtitle("Pred. probs. for k=2");
```

On the other hand, there is a clear interaction between jtyp and cat, with the human, and, to a lesser degrees, gAI judges making a distinction between the human- and AI-generated drawings, while the AI judges do not.

```{r}
# # jtyp & cat:
# if( !file.exists("./cache/glmer_mk2js.rds") )
# {
  mk2.js <- glmer(k2 ~ jtyp * subcat + (1 | iid) + (1 | jid), data=dclust$kmeans, family=binomial);
  #summary(mk2.js);
  mk2.js.noint <- update(mk2.js, . ~ . - jtyp:subcat); anova(mk2.js, mk2.js.noint); # the interaction clearly matters!
   saveRDS(mk2.js, file="./cache/glmer_mk2js.rds", compress="xz");
# } else
# {
#   mk2.js <- readRDS("./cache/glmer_mk2js.rds");
# }
summary(mk2.js);
anova(mk2.js, mk2.0); # and improves on the null model!
anova(mk2.js, mk2.j); # and on the judge-only model
plot_model(mk2.js, type="pred", terms=c("jtyp", "subcat")) + ylab("p(cluster 2)") + ggtitle("Pred. probs. for k=2");
```

Again, humans have a clear hierarchy of judgments, with HVA > HNA ≈ HAI > SAI, but the AI judge does not make any difference, while the gAI seem to follow the human hierarchy, most subcategories overlap a lot. 


#### How do the scale scores affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *liking* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k2), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *vividness* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k2), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *originality* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k2), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *aesthetics* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k2), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *curiosity* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k2), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k2 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

#### Summary

kmeans clustering with k=2 seems to suggest that the main distinction is between a cluster containing mostly human and gAI judgments that make distinctions between categories and subcategories, and a mostly AI judges-category does not make such distinctions.


### k=3 clusters

#### How do judge and category affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of responses by cluster and various criteria.")}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k3, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k3, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k3, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ cat) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k3, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k3, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ subcat) +
               geom_text(aes(x=k3, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ncol=3);
```

Cluster 1 seems to contain mostly the AI and gAI judgments, cluster 2 human and gAI, and 3 mostly the AI judgments.

#### How do the scale scores affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *liking* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k3), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *vividness* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k3), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *originality* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k3), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *aesthetics* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k3), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *curiosity* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k3), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k3 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

#### Correspondence k=2 ↔︎ k=3

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Correspondence between k=2 (colums) and k=3 (row) clusters.")}
# correspondence between k=2 and k=3 clusters
pander(tb_2_3 <- table(dclust$kmeans$k2, dclust$kmeans$k3));
mosaicplot(tb_2_3, color=TRUE, main="k=2 ↔︎ k=3", las=1);
```


### k=4 clusters

#### How do judge and category affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of responses by cluster and various criteria.")}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k4, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k4, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k4, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ cat) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k4, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k4, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ subcat) +
               geom_text(aes(x=k4, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ncol=3);
```

#### How do the scale scores affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *liking* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k4), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *vividness* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k4), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *originality* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k4), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *aesthetics* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k4), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *curiosity* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k4), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k4 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

#### Correspondence k=3 ↔︎ k=4

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Correspondence between k=3 (colums) and k=4 (row) clusters.")}
# correspondence between k=3 and k=4 clusters
pander(tb_3_4 <- table(dclust$kmeans$k3, dclust$kmeans$k4));
mosaicplot(tb_3_4, color=TRUE, main="k=3 ↔︎ k=4", las=1);
```


### k=5 clusters

#### How do judge and category affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of responses by cluster and various criteria.")}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k5, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k5, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k5, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ cat) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k5, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k5, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ subcat) +
               geom_text(aes(x=k5, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ncol=3);
```

#### How do the scale scores affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *liking* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k5), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *vividness* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k5), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *originality* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k5), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *aesthetics* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k5), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *curiosity* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k5), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k5 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

#### Correspondence k=4 ↔︎ k=5

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Correspondence between k=4 (colums) and k=5 (row) clusters.")}
# correspondence between k=4 and k=5 clusters
pander(tb_4_5 <- table(dclust$kmeans$k4, dclust$kmeans$k5));
mosaicplot(tb_4_5, color=TRUE, main="k=4 ↔︎ k=5", las=1);
```


### k=6 clusters

#### How do judge and category affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=3*5, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of responses by cluster and various criteria.")}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k6, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k6, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k6, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ cat) + 
               geom_text(aes(x=k6, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans) + 
               geom_bar(aes(x=k6, fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5) + facet_grid(. ~ subcat) +
               geom_text(aes(x=k6, group=jtyp, label=scales::percent(round(after_stat(count)/sum(after_stat(count)),2))), position=position_stack(vjust=0.5), stat="count") + 
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + xlab("k=2") + theme_bw() + 
               theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)),
             ncol=3);
```

#### How do the scale scores affect the distribution in the clusters?

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *liking* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k6), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=liking)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *vividness* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k6), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=vivid)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *originality* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k6), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=origin)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *aesthetics* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k6), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=aesth)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

```{r fig.width=2*5, fig.height=2*5, fig.cap=capFig("Distribution of *curiosity* scores by cluster and various criteria."), message=FALSE}
grid.arrange(ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
              geom_bar(aes(fill=k6), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) +
               scale_fill_discrete(name="Cluster") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("All judges and categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ .) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw()  + ggtitle("By judge across categories") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ cat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and category") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ggplot(dclust$kmeans, aes(x=curios)) + 
               geom_bar(aes(fill=jtyp), color="black", alpha=0.5, position=position_dodge()) + facet_grid(k6 ~ subcat) +
               scale_fill_manual(values=c("human"="steelblue", "AI"="orange", "gAI"="red"), name="Judge") + theme_bw() + ggtitle("By judge and subcategory") + theme(legend.position="bottom"),
             ncol=2);
```

#### Correspondence k=5 ↔︎ k=6

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=5, fig.cap=capFig("Correspondence between k=5 (colums) and k=6 (row) clusters.")}
# correspondence between k=5 and k=6 clusters
pander(tb_5_6 <- table(dclust$kmeans$k5, dclust$kmeans$k6));
mosaicplot(tb_5_6, color=TRUE, main="k=5 ↔︎ k=6", las=1);
```

### Summary

Using k-means clustering with the number of clusters *k* between 2 and 6 seems to suggest that the main distinction is really between human and AI judgments, with gAI somewhere in between somewhat more simialr to human than to AI, and then mostly between the portions of the scales used.

```{r echo=FALSE, results='hide'}
rm(dclust); gc();
```


## Item properties

Let's now look at the properties of the 5 measures using Mokken Scaling Analysis (MSA), PCA and FA.
For more details, please see @dima_scale_2018 and the accompanying `R` code available at [https://github.com/alexadima/6-steps-protocol](https://github.com/alexadima/6-steps-protocol).
We will do it for the whole data, as well as for the human and AI judgements separately, when the case.


### Mokken Scaling Analysis (MSA)

```{r, fig.width=4, fig.height=4, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, fig.cap=capFig("MSA: histogram of the number of Guttman errors (gPlus) across all items.")}
# Outliers:
xPlus   <- rowSums(dall[,c("liking", "vivid", "origin", "aesth", "curios")]);
if( !file.exists("./cache/gErrors.rds") ) # computationally expensive
{
